question: How to set the camera model to \P\ model?
content:
1. Turn the Mode Dial until the index points to

. 2. This selects Program AE mode: P stands for Program and AE stands for Auto Exposure. In this mode, the camera automatically sets the shutter speed and aperture to suit the subject's brightness.
ret:
"1. Turn the Mode Dial until the index points to

.\n2. This selects Program AE mode: P stands for Program and AE stands for Auto Exposure. In this mode, the camera automatically sets the shutter speed and aperture to suit the subject's brightness.",["Manual10_116"]
notes: The question wording says model, but the Canon EOS 20D evidence identifies P as Program AE mode on the Mode Dial. One image is used because it shows the Mode Dial set to P.
